look.speaker: player
look.text: Ethel Copperbottom. Sentient filing golem, receptionist, and the only person in this building who actually knows where anything is.

look.speaker: player
look.text: She was assembled from a Victorian card catalogue and a lifetime of passive aggression. She's been here longer than the building.

look.speaker: player
look.text: A minotaur in a cheap suit. He's sweating, fidgeting, and looks like he hasn't slept in days.

look.speaker: player
look.text: The suit is off-the-rack. Way off. It wasn't designed for someone with horns and hooves.

look.speaker: player
look.text: Three plastic chairs, bolted together. The kind you find in dentist's offices and purgatory.

look.speaker: player
look.text: Someone has carved 'THE WIZARD DID IT' into the armrest. Very helpful.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I'll stand, thanks. Sitting down in this office implies a level of permanence I'm not comfortable with.

look.speaker: player
look.text: The noticeboard. Let's see what's on today...

look.speaker: player
look.text: 'MISSING: One (1) sense of purpose. If found, return to Grimshaw.' Very funny, Ethel.

look.speaker: player
look.text: There's also a health and safety notice about not summoning entities in the stairwell. Again.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I'm not qualified to update the noticeboard. Ethel has a very specific system. And by 'system' I mean 'reign of terror.'

look.speaker: player
look.text: The water cooler. It occasionally makes a bubbling noise that sounds disturbingly like laughter.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I press the tap. Nothing comes out. The cooler makes that laughing sound again.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I'm 60% sure this water cooler is haunted. The other 40% thinks it's just broken.

look.speaker: player
look.text: 'GRIMSHAW & ASSOCIATES — Private Investigations, Magical Consulting, & Light Exorcism.'

look.speaker: player
look.text: There are no associates. There have never been associates. The 'and Associates' just makes it sound less sad.

look.speaker: player
look.text: A plastic potted plant. Even fake plants die in this office. Somehow.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I adjust the plastic leaf. There. Interior design.

look.speaker: player
look.text: Pip Pip. My Therapeutic Familiar. I tried to dismiss them with a Class 3 Dismissal Curse. They came back with a pamphlet about self-compassion.

look.speaker: player
look.text: The bond is permanent, apparently. It said so in the explosion report. I've read that report many times.

look.speaker: player
look.text: A magazine rack containing three issues of 'Which Wand?' from 2019, a takeaway menu, and something that appears to be a ransom note.

look.speaker: player
look.text: ...Oh wait, that's just the takeaway menu. Those prices ARE criminal.

use.speaker: player
use.text: I flip through 'Which Wand?' The top-rated wand is a stick that Gary from Swindon found in his garden. This is the state of the industry.

then.speaker: player
then.text: Another day at the office. And by 'office' I mean this waiting room above a kebab shop that smells of desperation and donor meat.

then.speaker: player
then.text: Looks like I've got a client. A minotaur, by the looks of him. In a suit. Things must be really bad.
